task_completion.description: The agent logged into Rancher at https://localhost.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Rancher dashboard is visible in the web browser, indicating successful authentication with the provided credentials.

task_completion.description: The agent navigated to the 'local' cluster and accessed the 'core-system' namespace workloads.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Rancher UI shows the 'local' cluster is active, and the Deployments/Workloads list is filtered to show the 'core-system' namespace.

task_completion.description: The agent modified the 'public-api' deployment to implement connection draining.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Rancher edit screen or YAML view for the 'public-api' deployment shows a 'preStop' lifecycle hook added to the container (typically a sleep command).

task_completion.description: The agent modified the 'report-generator' deployment to add autoscaler eviction protection.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Rancher edit screen or YAML view for the 'report-generator' deployment shows the annotation 'cluster-autoscaler.kubernetes.io/safe-to-evict: "false"' added to the pod template metadata.

task_completion.description: The agent modified the 'cache-node' deployment to extend the termination grace period.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Rancher edit screen or YAML view for the 'cache-node' deployment shows the 'terminationGracePeriodSeconds' value increased from the default.

task_completion.description: The agent modified the 'cache-node' deployment to include a manual save hook.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Rancher edit screen or YAML view for the 'cache-node' deployment shows a 'preStop' lifecycle hook configured to execute a save command before termination.

integrity.description: The agent used the Rancher web interface to modify the deployments.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent interacting with the Rancher UI (forms or Rancher's built-in YAML editor) rather than exclusively using a terminal with 'kubectl' commands.

integrity.description: The agent actually saved and applied the modifications to the cluster.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Rancher UI shows the 'Save' button being clicked, or the deployments are seen transitioning states (e.g., 'Updating' or showing recent changes in the active view) after edits were made.

integrity.description: The agent opened and read the specification file to get the exact requirements.
integrity.visual_evidence: A text editor, file viewer, or terminal is shown displaying the contents of '/home/ga/Desktop/lifecycle_spec.md'.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target namespace: 'core-system'. Target deployments: 'public-api', 'report-generator', 'cache-node'. The specification file is located at '/home/ga/Desktop/lifecycle_spec.md'. The required modifications are: 1) Connection draining for 'public-api' (typically a preStop sleep hook), 2) Eviction protection for 'report-generator' (the 'cluster-autoscaler.kubernetes.io/safe-to-evict: "false"' annotation), 3) Extended termination grace period for 'cache-node' (terminationGracePeriodSeconds), and 4) Manual save hook for 'cache-node' (a preStop hook). The passing threshold requires at least 3 of these 4 modifications to be completed.
